headline: Berkshire is extra bullish on Delta, Alphabet as CEO adjusts portfolio
summary: Berkshire Hathaway (BRK-A, BRK-B) has adjusted its equity portfolio under new CEO Greg Abel, including a new $2.6 billion stake in Delta Air Lines (DAL) while tripling the firm's stake in Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL). The Glenview Trust Company CIO Bill Stone takes a closer look at Berkshire's revamped equity holdings and which stocks it has dumped under Abel. After stepping down from the chief executive role at the beginning of 2026, Warren Buffett remains as Berkshire's executive chairman.

headline: Following Buffett: Greg Abel Cuts Berkshire Holdings Dating Back To 2011  — But The Big Names Stay Put
summary: Berkshire Hathaway changed its holdings in the first quarter, but its top 10 stocks look the same from the fourth quarter.

headline: Berkshire CEO Greg Abel sells 16 stocks, triples Alphabet
summary: Greg Abel exited positions including Visa, Mastercard, Amazon, and UnitedHealth while boosting Berkshire's Alphabet stake to almost 58 million shares

headline: Berkshire Hathaway exits UnitedHealth stake Q1 2026
summary: The exit was part of a broader portfolio reshuffle in the first quarter after Greg Abel succeeded Warren Buffett as CEO

headline: Domino's® to Help Ease the Pain of a U.S. Red Card by Giving Away $1 Million Worth of Emergency Pizzas
summary: Domino's is gearing up for soccer's biggest games this summer, in more ways than one: with free Emergency Pizzas and exclusive merchandise! If any player from the U.S. team receives a red card in the soccer tournament, Domino's is here to help soften the blow by giving away $1 million worth of Emergency Pizzas. headline: One Lane Closed. The Whole Rate Picture Changed
summary: One shipping lane controls roughly 20 percent of the world's oil supply. Right now, it's closed. And in the space of five trading days, that single fact has moved oil to $105 a barrel, pushed Treasury yields to their highest point in roughly a year, and resurrected a rate hike scenario that nobody was pricing a week ago. Welcome to Monday.
